---
name: Template proposal
about: Propose a sanctioned template evolution without smuggling in a spec change
title: "[Template] "
labels: template
assignees: ""
---

## Repository pressure

Describe the repository shape, workflow pressure, or drift condition that the
current templates do not handle well.

## Why current templates are insufficient

Explain why `templates/standard/genesis.md`, `templates/complex/genesis.md`,
and the current snippets cannot address this case through the existing
configurable boundary.

## Template issue or spec issue?

- [ ] This stays inside the current invariant framework contract.
- [ ] This may actually require a spec or governance change.

Explain why.

## Proposed template adaptation

Describe the proposed shape, added sections, or structural emphasis. Be precise
about what changes in the template and what does not change in the framework.

## Contract preservation

Explain how the proposal preserves:

- the private, local-only continuity memory boundary
- the mandatory three-layer core
- mandatory Present Wizard
- mandatory Layer 3 as an operating manual

## Downstream review impact

List any surfaces that would need review if this proposal were adopted:

- [ ] Templates only
- [ ] Examples
- [ ] Schema
- [ ] Adoption surfaces
- [ ] Contributor surfaces
- [ ] Philosophy / Spec / Governance

## Evidence

Link the proposal to a real class of repositories or a concrete adoption
failure. Do not argue from taste alone.
